source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Working With Jinja Templates: Building complete files with loops and data structures

questions.q: In a Jinja template, how do you loop over a list?
questions.why: {% for %} / {% endfor %} is the Jinja control block for iteration.

questions.q: Which templating engine does the template module use?
questions.why: The template module leverages the Jinja2 engine.

questions.q: components is a list of dicts with keys name/path/port. How is a component's port referenced?
questions.why: Dot access on the loop item: {{ component.port }}.

questions.q: Beyond simple substitution, what advanced features does Jinja2 give in templates?
questions.why: Jinja2 provides conditionals, loops, and filters for advanced template manipulation.

questions.q: Which is a recommended best practice for templates?
options: Keep config logic in the templates (separation of concerns) and version-control them
questions.why: Separate the logic into templates, store them under version control, and test them independently.

questions.q: A key benefit of the template module for configuration management is that it...
options: Separates configuration logic from data, making playbooks flexible and maintainable
questions.why: It separates logic and data, improving flexibility and maintainability.
